questions.question: What is the topic for Week 30?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Thirty is CRISIS MANAGEMENT.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 29?
questions.explanation: Coping with Loss was the topic for Week 29.

questions.question: God is our refuge and ________, A very present help in trouble.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Psalm 46:1) says 'God is our refuge and strength...'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Psalm 46:1.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 30 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: God is our refuge and strength, A very present help in trouble.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Psalm 46:1.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Psalm 46:1), what is God to us in times of trouble?
questions.explanation: The verse says God is 'A very present help in trouble.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, what is a crisis?
options: B. A time of great joy
C. An inevitable negative event that leads to a dangerous or unstable situation
D. An opportunity to make money easily
questions.explanation: The introduction states 'A crisis is an inevitable negative event that leads to a dangerous or unstable situation (Job 14:1).'

questions.question: According to the lesson, being born again is a guarantee of a jolly ride on our journey of faith with no crises.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states 'For a child of God, being born again is no guarantee of a jolly ride on our journey of faith.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'FORMS OF CRISIS'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, which of the following is considered a 'Spiritual Crisis'?
options: B. An experience that challenges one's faith or sense of purpose
questions.explanation: Point 1 describes Spiritual Crisis as 'an experience that challenges one’s faith or sense of purpose and a time when one seeks deeper understanding...'

questions.question: What form of crisis may come from physical illness or the death of a loved one?
questions.explanation: Point 2 says 'Health crisis: This may come in the form of physical illness or death of a loved one...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding financial crisis (severe financial distress)?
questions.explanation: Point 3 cites '(Mark 5:25-26; 2 Kings 4:1)'.

questions.question: What type of crisis arises from divergent opinions and conflicts of interest while relating with one another in society?
questions.explanation: Point 4 describes 'Social crisis: Disagreements are bound to arise from divergent opinions and sometimes conflict of interest while relating with one another in society...'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'HOW TO MANAGE CRISIS'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, what is wise to do regarding foreseeable crises?
questions.explanation: The text states 'It is wise to prevent foreseeable crisis (Proverbs 27:12).'

questions.question: When an unforeseen crisis occurs, the lesson advises to immediately attempt to solve it without first defining its nature or causes.
questions.explanation: The text says '...it is important to seek to properly understand the crisis by defining its nature, causes and implications... before attempting to solve it.'

questions.question: According to the lesson, every crisis is an opportunity to prove yourself as a what?
questions.explanation: The text says '...every crisis is an opportunity to prove yourself as a solution provider (Genesis 41:32-41).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding praying and communicating problems and solutions to elicit contributions (like Queen Esther did)?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Esther 4:13-17)'.

questions.question: According to the conclusion, believers should ensure the safety and wellbeing of all concerned in a crisis and that God is glorified.
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states this.

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'So we built the wall, and the entire wall was joined together up to half its height, for the people had a mind to work.'
questions.verse_text: So we built the wall, and the entire wall was joined together up to half its height, for the people had a mind to work.
questions.explanation: This is verse 6 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Nehemiah 4:7), what was the reaction of Sanballat and Tobiah when they heard the walls were being restored?
questions.explanation: Verse 7 says '...they became very angry.'

questions.question: In Nehemiah 4:8, what did the enemies conspire to do?
options: A. Pay the builders to stop
B. Come and attack Jerusalem and create confusion
C. Burn the city gates secretly
questions.explanation: Verse 8 says 'and all of them conspired together to come and attack Jerusalem and create confusion.'

questions.question: According to Nehemiah 4:9, how did Nehemiah and the people respond to the threat?
options: B. They made their prayer to God and set a watch day and night
C. They paid the enemies a tribute
questions.explanation: Verse 9 says 'Nevertheless we made our prayer to our God, and because of them we set a watch against them day and night.'

questions.question: According to Nehemiah 4:17, how did those who built on the wall and carried burdens equip themselves?
options: C. With one hand they worked at construction, and with the other held a weapon
D. They hired mercenaries to guard them
questions.explanation: Verse 17 says '...so that with one hand they worked at construction, and with the other held a weapon.'

questions.question: According to Nehemiah 4:18, where did every one of the builders have his sword?
options: B. In a scabbard on his back
C. Laying on the ground nearby
questions.explanation: Verse 18 says 'Every one of the builders had his sword girded at his side as he built.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that for believers, it is not every crisis that is negative?
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'For believers, it is not every crisis that is negative (Romans 8:28).'

questions.question: Domestic violence, wayward children, and infidelity are given as examples of Family crisis.
questions.explanation: Point 5 lists 'divorce, infidelity, wayward children, domestic violence, etc.' under Family crisis.

questions.question: According to the lesson, after taking action during a crisis, what should you do next?
options: C. Monitor and review the progress on the actions taken
questions.explanation: Outline II concludes with 'Monitor and review the progress on the actions taken (Nehemiah 4:19-23).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding being willing to declare your God-inspired convictions during a crisis?
questions.explanation: Outline II cites '(Nehemiah 1:1-4; 2:1-5)' for declaring convictions.

questions.question: How we respond to these crises matters as it is a clear reflection of how deeply we trust ________ and have faith in His word.
questions.explanation: The introduction says '...it is a clear reflection of how deeply we trust God and have faith in His word.'
